mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no significant negative correlation with rate levels.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate above 4.0 percent signals immediate labor market deterioration and weakening consumer spending, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in more than 55 percent of triggered instances over a 5-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between real GDP growth and real spending growth, where GDPC1 rises while RSAFS declines, indicates structural weakening in consumer demand that suppresses corporate revenues and produces negative returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses statistical significance with p-value greater than 0.10 in out-of-sample regression models.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index values exceeding 108 signal severe supply-side capacity constraints that compress profit margins and trigger negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 60 percent of high-INDPRO periods during supply-constrained expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rate combined with stable unemployment rate indicates a successful soft-landing policy path that preserves earnings growth while controlling inflation, supporting positive equity returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Interaction effect reverses sign or becomes statistically insignificant in walk-forward validation tests.

mechanism_narrative: Higher housing starts indicate strong near-term construction demand and broad economic momentum that directly boosts corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with housing starts.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth exceeding 250 thousand signals labor market overheating that accelerates rate hike expectations, prompting negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in more than 50 percent of triggered months over a 5-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation exceeds 3.0 percent and the federal funds rate remains below inflation, negative real rates fuel asset price inflation and generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power and Rank IC drops below 0.02 when real rates turn positive.
